name: Mini-Cassia Empl.
description_short: Marathon County Employees Credit Union (MCECU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union based in Wausau, WI, offering savings accounts, loans, and financial services to eligible members.
description_long:
Marathon County Employees Credit Union (MCECU) is a federally chartered credit union serving the Wausau, Wisconsin area. Based on its website, the institution operates as a traditional member-owned financial cooperative providing a full range of banking and lending products to its membership base.

MCECU offers a comprehensive suite of savings products including regular savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), health savings accounts, individual retirement accounts (IRAs), and specialized accounts like Christmas Club and Funeral Trusts. On the lending side, they provide auto loans for new and used vehicles, recreational vehicle financing, mortgage loans, home equity loans, debt consolidation loans, signature loans, and lines of credit. The credit union also issues Visa credit cards and offers checking accounts tailored to different member segments (regular, 55+, student, and small business).

What distinguishes MCECU is its emphasis on member convenience and financial counseling. The institution operates a Lake View branch with limited hours (Tuesdays and Thursdays, 11am-3pm), maintains surcharge-free ATM access through shared branching networks, and offers modern digital services including mobile banking, remote deposit capture, online bill pay, and a 24-hour telephone teller service. They explicitly market financial counselors on staff to help members improve their financial situations. The credit union also handles specialty services like foreign currency exchange, savings bonds redemption, and free notary services.

As a credit union, MCECU's primary limitation is membership eligibility—not all consumers can join. The website does not clearly specify membership requirements, though the name suggests an employee-based affiliation. Limited branch hours at the Lake View location may inconvenience some members, and the institution appears to serve a regional rather than national market. Like most smaller credit unions, MCECU may have more limited product sophistication compared to larger national institutions.
services: Regular savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit
Individual Retirement Accounts (Roth, Conversion Roth, SEP, Regular, Education)
Auto loans for new and used vehicles
Recreational vehicle and motor home financing
Mortgage loans and home equity loans
Debt consolidation loans and lines of credit
Online bill pay and mobile banking (iOS/Android apps)
Remote deposit capture (RDC) for mobile check deposits
24-hour telephone teller and MARS member audio response system
Free notary services and check reordering
pros: Offers financial counselors on-site to help members improve financial situations
Provides surcharge-free ATM access through nationwide shared branching network
Low loan rates advertised starting at 3.74% APR
Full suite of savings vehicles including HSAs, IRAs, and specialty savings accounts
Modern digital banking including mobile app, remote deposit capture, and 24-hour telephone teller
Member-owned not-for-profit structure returns profits to members as dividends
Diverse loan products including auto, RV, mortgage, home equity, and debt consolidation
cons: Limited physical branch access—Lake View branch only open Tuesdays and Thursdays 11am-3pm
Membership eligibility restrictions not clearly disclosed on website (appears to be employee-based)
Regional rather than national institution, limiting convenience for geographically dispersed members
No explicit information about minimum account balances, fee structures, or dividend rates disclosed on main pages
Small institution may lack some advanced products and services available at larger credit unions
best_for: Eligible employees seeking membership-based financial services with personalized counseling
Local Wausau-area residents wanting to support a not-for-profit financial cooperative
Members prioritizing financial education and on-site counselor access
Borrowers seeking competitive loan rates and multiple lending product options
diagnosis: MCECU is best suited for eligible members in the Marathon County, Wisconsin area seeking member-owned banking with competitive rates, personalized financial counseling, and modern digital access. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—prospective customers must verify they meet the credit union's membership requirements, which are not transparently stated on the website but appear employment-related.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
